top_signals.title: Outgunned, But Not Outplayed: Iran’s Theory of Victory
top_signals.summary:
Nineteen weeks ago, Iran faced the combined might of the most powerful country in the world and the most advanced military in the Middle East. Today, it is dictating the terms of the peace.When the memorandum of understanding was signed last month, President Donald Trump declared, “The Deal with the Islamic Republic of Iran is now complete. Congratulations to all!” and told the ships of the world to start their engines. Iran’s response was to keep striking and impose toll collection on the world’s most critical shipping lane while expanding its influence in Iraq, where it is increasingly seen as

The post Outgunned, But Not Outplayed: Iran’s Theory of Victory appeared first on War on the Rocks.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Microsoft Patches a Record 570 Security Flaws
top_signals.summary: Microsoft Corp. today released software updates to plug at least 570 security holes in its Windows operating systems and other software, almost triple the number of vulnerabilities the software giant fixed in its record-smashing Patch Tuesday release last month. Microsoft attributed the burgeoning patch counts to vulnerability discoveries aided by artificial intelligence.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Agents of change − speech by Sarah Breeden
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Navy awards TOTE $2.2 billion to manage Medium Landing Ships project
top_signals.summary: The Navy awarded TOTE $2.2 billion to manage its medium landing ship construction efforts, with the first vessel scheduled for delivery in fall 2029.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Trump blasts New York AI data center ban, says state should change policy 'immediately'
top_signals.summary: New York became the first state in the U.S. to impose a ban of its kind, after the state executive order was signed by the governor Tuesday.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Before the Next Mythos Moment: The Case for an AI Threat Fusion Center
top_signals.summary:
Earlier this spring, Claude’s developers found the cyber potential of Anthropic’s model, Mythos 5, hazardous enough to pump the brakes on model release, creating the Project Glasswing collaboration to give cyber defenders the upper hand through controlled access to the model. Despite these efforts, the U.S. government stepped in on June 12, 2026, and forced Anthropic to take down Mythos and its safeguarded form, Fable 5 — an action the company disputed, calling the trigger a narrow, nonuniversal jailbreak. A lack of standard processes, complex legal terrain, and information-sharing obstacles resulted in a “pickup game” where, absent a shared factual

The post Before the Next Mythos Moment: The Case for an AI Threat Fusion Center appeared first on War on the Rocks.

top_signals.title: Revolut receives in-principle approval from UAE authorities for crypto services
top_signals.summary: Dubai’s Virtual Assets Regulatory Authority approved the London-headquartered fintech company for broker-dealer, management and investment, and exchange services in the UAE.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: Is Robinhood Chain’s success bullish or bearish for ETH the asset?
top_signals.summary: Surging volumes on Robinhood Chain could be very good for Ethereum, but only if the “ETH is money” crowd turn out to be right.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.
